questions.objective_ref: Define what an API (Application Programming Interface) is, using the 'interface' idea to explain what the word means.
Explain the request-response cycle: a client sends a request, a server processes it, and the server sends back a response.
Identify the four components of an API request: endpoint (URL), HTTP method, headers, and body.
Identify the three components of an API response: status code, headers, and body.
Explain why LLM models like Claude are accessed through an API and how your Python code fits into that picture as the client.
Explain using the waiter analogy why an API exists as an abstraction layer between your code and the server.
